title: Thelypteris platyptera
taxonomy: Thelypteris platyptera is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which is a group of vascular plants with fern-like leaves. The Thelypteridaceae family is part of the order Polypodiales, which is a large group of ferns that have two distinct leaf veins. Thelypteris platyptera is native to the Americas, where it can be found growing in a variety of habitats. This species is often classified as a type of deciduous or semi-evergreen fern, depending on the region and environmental conditions.
uses: Thelypteris platyptera is a species of fern that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves are known to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
